theater_description: Nine-month campaign to recapture Mosul from ISIS.
geometry_method_note: Uses citywide battle-space framing rather than neighborhood geometry.
military_area_note: No publishable all-side combatant strength/exposure series retained.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator withheld because displacement and flight were too dynamic in the retained source chain to support a stable person-month series.
split_reason: Retains the main campaign row and adds side-specific civilian subsets where sources distinguish coalition, Iraqi forces, or ISIS.
quality_note: Good multi-source civilian fatality evidence and several side-attributed subsets, but weak denominator and combatant coverage.

theater_description: Eastern half of the Mosul operation through declared liberation of East Mosul.
military_area_note: No combatant strength/exposure series retained for the east phase.
civilian_area_note: No civilian exposure denominator retained because the source chain does not support a stable east-side exposed-population series.
split_reason: Phase retained for continuity even though the strongest retained numeric side-attribution evidence is campaign-wide or west-phase-specific.
quality_note: Phase row preserved, but published side-specific totals for the east phase are weak in the retained source chain.

theater_description: Assault on densely populated western Mosul through the city's recapture.
geometry_method_note: No neighborhood-level geometry retained in this retrofit.
civilian_area_note: No civilian exposure denominator retained because trapped-population and flight numbers move too quickly in the retained source base.
split_reason: Retained because the strongest side-attributed civilian reporting concentrates in western Mosul.
quality_note: High-value row for side-attributed civilian harm, but battle-space denominators remain too unstable.
